req.title: KYC refresh (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for KYC Refresh Requirement
Requirement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days;
             accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to
             inbound transactions only.
Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (minimal stubs – replace with real imports from your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AccountStatus(Enum):
    ACTIVE = auto()
    KYC_RESTRICTED = auto()   # beyond refresh window → inbound only
    SUSPENDED = auto()
    CLOSED = auto()


class TransactionType(Enum):
    INBOUND = auto()    # credit / deposit / incoming transfer
    OUTBOUND = auto()   # debit / withdrawal / outgoing transfer


class TransactionStatus(Enum):
    APPROVED = auto()
    DECLINED = auto()


class DeclineReason(Enum):
    KYC_REFRESH_REQUIRED = auto()
    INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS = auto()
    UNAUTHORISED = auto()
    LIMIT_EXCEEDED = auto()
    ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED = auto()
    ACCOUNT_CLOSED = auto()


@dataclass
class Customer:
    customer_id: str
    name: str
    last_kyc_refresh: datetime


@dataclass
class Account:
    account_id: str
    customer: Customer
    balance: Decimal
    status: AccountStatus = AccountStatus.ACTIVE
    daily_outbound_limit: Decimal = Decimal("10000.00")
    daily_outbound_used: Decimal = Decimal("0.00")


@dataclass
class TransactionRequest:
    account: Account
    transaction_type: TransactionType
    amount: Decimal
    initiated_by: str          # customer_id or "SYSTEM" / agent id
    reference: str = ""


@dataclass
class TransactionResult:
    status: TransactionStatus
    decline_reason: Optional[DeclineReason] = None
    new_balance: Optional[Decimal] = None
    message: str = ""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (minimal implementation – replace with real service)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

KYC_REFRESH_WINDOW_DAYS = 365


class KYCRefreshError(Exception):
    pass


class InsufficientFundsError(Exception):
    pass


class UnauthorisedError(Exception):
    pass


class LimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass


class AccountService:
    """Thin service layer that enforces KYC refresh and transaction rules."""

    def __init__(self, clock=None):
        # Allow injecting a clock for deterministic tests
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # KYC helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def is_kyc_current(self, customer: Customer) -> bool:
        age = (self._clock() - customer.last_kyc_refresh).days
        return age <= KYC_REFRESH_WINDOW_DAYS

    def days_since_kyc(self, customer: Customer) -> int:
        return (self._clock() - customer.last_kyc_refresh).days

    def apply_kyc_status(self, account: Account) -> Account:
        """Re-evaluate and update account status based on KYC freshness."""
        if account.status in (AccountStatus.SUSPENDED, AccountStatus.CLOSED):
            return account  # other statuses take precedence
        if not self.is_kyc_current(account.customer):
            account.status = AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED
        else:
            if account.status == AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED:
                account.status = AccountStatus.ACTIVE
        return account

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Transaction processing
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def process_transaction(self, request: TransactionRequest) -> TransactionResult:
        account = request.account
        amount = request.amount

        # 1. Authorisation check – only the account owner or SYSTEM may transact
        if request.initiated_by not in (account.customer.customer_id, "SYSTEM"):
            return TransactionResult(
                status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                decline_reason=DeclineReason.UNAUTHORISED,
                message="Initiator is not authorised to transact on this account.",
            )

        # 2. Account status checks
        if account.status == AccountStatus.CLOSED:
            return TransactionResult(
                status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                decline_reason=DeclineReason.ACCOUNT_CLOSED,
                message="Account is closed.",
            )

        if account.status == AccountStatus.SUSPENDED:
            return TransactionResult(
                status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                decline_reason=DeclineReason.ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED,
                message="Account is suspended.",
            )

        # 3. KYC restriction – only inbound transactions are allowed
        self.apply_kyc_status(account)
        if account.status == AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED:
            if request.transaction_type == TransactionType.OUTBOUND:
                return TransactionResult(
                    status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                    decline_reason=DeclineReason.KYC_REFRESH_REQUIRED,
                    message="KYC refresh required. Outbound transactions are blocked.",
                )
            # Inbound is still allowed even when KYC_RESTRICTED

        # 4. Outbound-specific checks
        if request.transaction_type == TransactionType.OUTBOUND:
            # 4a. Insufficient funds
            if amount > account.balance:
                return TransactionResult(
                    status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                    decline_reason=DeclineReason.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS,
                    message=f"Insufficient funds. Balance: {account.balance}, Requested: {amount}",
                )
            # 4b. Daily limit
            if account.daily_outbound_used + amount > account.daily_outbound_limit:
                return TransactionResult(
                    status=TransactionStatus.DECLINED,
                    decline_reason=DeclineReason.LIMIT_EXCEEDED,
                    message=(
                        f"Daily outbound limit exceeded. "
                        f"Limit: {account.daily_outbound_limit}, "
                        f"Used: {account.daily_outbound_used}, "
                        f"Requested: {amount}"
                    ),
                )
            # Debit
            account.balance -= amount
            account.daily_outbound_used += amount
        else:
            # Inbound – credit unconditionally (after status checks above)
            account.balance += amount

        return TransactionResult(
            status=TransactionStatus.APPROVED,
            new_balance=account.balance,
            message="Transaction approved.",
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOW = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def fixed_clock():
    return NOW


@pytest.fixture
def service():
    return AccountService(clock=fixed_clock)


def _make_customer(days_since_refresh: int, customer_id: str = "CUST-001") -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id=customer_id,
        name="Jane Doe",
        last_kyc_refresh=NOW - timedelta(days=days_since_refresh),
    )


def _make_account(
    customer: Customer,
    balance: Decimal = Decimal("5000.00"),
    status: AccountStatus = AccountStatus.ACTIVE,
    daily_limit: Decimal = Decimal("10000.00"),
    daily_used: Decimal = Decimal("0.00"),
) -> Account:
    return Account(
        account_id="ACC-001",
        customer=customer,
        balance=balance,
        status=status,
        daily_outbound_limit=daily_limit,
        daily_outbound_used=daily_used,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

JUST_WITHIN_WINDOW = 365   # exactly 365 days → still current
ONE_DAY_OVERDUE    = 366   # 366 days → expired
FAR_OVERDUE        = 730   # 2 years overdue


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 1 – KYC Freshness Logic
# ===========================================================================

class TestKYCFreshness:
    """Unit tests for is_kyc_current() and days_since_kyc()."""

    def test_kyc_current_same_day(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=0)
        assert service.is_kyc_current(customer) is True

    def test_kyc_current_at_boundary_365_days(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=JUST_WITHIN_WINDOW)
        assert service.is_kyc_current(customer) is True

    def test_kyc_expired_at_366_days(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        assert service.is_kyc_current(customer) is False

    def test_kyc_expired_far_overdue(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=FAR_OVERDUE)
        assert service.is_kyc_current(customer) is False

    def test_days_since_kyc_accuracy(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=200)
        assert service.days_since_kyc(customer) == 200

    def test_kyc_expired_just_past_midnight(self, service):
        """366 days minus one second is still 365 full days; boundary edge."""
        customer = Customer(
            customer_id="CUST-EDG",
            name="Edge Case",
            last_kyc_refresh=NOW - timedelta(days=366, seconds=-1),
        )
        # days attribute of timedelta truncates to 365 full days
        assert service.days_since_kyc(customer) == 365
        assert service.is_kyc_current(customer) is True


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 2 – Account Status After KYC Evaluation
# ===========================================================================

class TestApplyKYCStatus:

    def test_active_account_stays_active_when_kyc_current(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=100)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        service.apply_kyc_status(account)
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_active_account_becomes_restricted_when_kyc_expired(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        service.apply_kyc_status(account)
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED

    def test_restricted_account_restored_after_kyc_refreshed(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, status=AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED)
        # Simulate customer completing KYC refresh
        customer.last_kyc_refresh = NOW
        service.apply_kyc_status(account)
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_suspended_account_not_overridden_by_kyc_logic(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, status=AccountStatus.SUSPENDED)
        service.apply_kyc_status(account)
        # SUSPENDED should not be downgraded to KYC_RESTRICTED
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.SUSPENDED

    def test_closed_account_not_overridden_by_kyc_logic(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, status=AccountStatus.CLOSED)
        service.apply_kyc_status(account)
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.CLOSED


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 3 – Happy Path Transactions (KYC current)
# ===========================================================================

class TestHappyPathTransactions:

    def test_outbound_approved_when_kyc_current(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=100)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("1000.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("200.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("800.00")

    def test_inbound_approved_when_kyc_current(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=100)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("1000.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("500.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("1500.00")

    def test_outbound_at_exact_daily_limit_approved(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=100)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("0.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("10000.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_daily_used_accumulates_correctly(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=100)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("4000.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("6000.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert account.daily_outbound_used == Decimal("10000.00")

    def test_inbound_approved_by_system_initiator(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=50)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="SYSTEM",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 4 – KYC Restriction Enforcement
# ===========================================================================

class TestKYCRestrictionEnforcement:

    def test_outbound_blocked_when_kyc_expired(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("5000.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.KYC_REFRESH_REQUIRED

    def test_inbound_allowed_when_kyc_expired(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("1000.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("250.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("1250.00")

    def test_inbound_allowed_when_far_overdue(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=FAR_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("50.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("50.00")

    def test_outbound_blocked_far_overdue(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=FAR_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("9999.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("1.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.KYC_REFRESH_REQUIRED

    def test_outbound_approved_immediately_after_kyc_refresh(self, service):
        """Completing KYC refresh at day 366 should re-enable outbound."""
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("5000.00"))

        # Simulate KYC refresh
        customer.last_kyc_refresh = NOW
        account.status = AccountStatus.ACTIVE

        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("500.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED

    def test_multiple_inbound_transactions_allowed_in_restricted_state(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
        for amount in [Decimal("100.00"), Decimal("200.00"), Decimal("50.00")]:
            req = TransactionRequest(
                account=account,
                transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
                amount=amount,
                initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
            )
            result = service.process_transaction(req)
            assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert account.balance == Decimal("350.00")

    def test_error_message_contains_kyc_context(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert "KYC" in result.message or "kyc" in result.message.lower()


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 5 – Negative Path: Insufficient Funds
# ===========================================================================

class TestInsufficientFunds:

    def test_outbound_declined_when_amount_exceeds_balance(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("99.99"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS

    def test_outbound_declined_on_zero_balance(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("0.01"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS

    def test_balance_unchanged_after_insufficient_funds_decline(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        original_balance = Decimal("50.00")
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=original_balance)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("200.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        service.process_transaction(req)
        assert account.balance == original_balance

    def test_insufficient_funds_message_contains_balance_info(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("50.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("999.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
        assert "50.00" in result.message or "999.00" in result.message

    def test_inbound_to_zero_balance_account_succeeds(self, service):
        """Inbound should not require funds; it's a credit."""
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("0.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("1000.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.new_balance == Decimal("1000.00")


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 6 – Negative Path: Unauthorised Initiator
# ===========================================================================

class TestUnauthorisedInitiator:

    def test_third_party_customer_id_rejected(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="CUST-FRAUD",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.UNAUTHORISED

    def test_empty_initiator_rejected(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.UNAUTHORISED

    def test_system_initiator_allowed_for_inbound(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.INBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="SYSTEM",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED

    def test_system_initiator_allowed_for_outbound(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=Decimal("1000.00"))
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="SYSTEM",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED

    def test_unauthorised_does_not_mutate_balance(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        original_balance = Decimal("5000.00")
        account = _make_account(customer, balance=original_balance)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("500.00"),
            initiated_by="ATTACKER-99",
        )
        service.process_transaction(req)
        assert account.balance == original_balance

    def test_unauthorised_on_kyc_expired_account(self, service):
        """Unauthorised check should fire before KYC check."""
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=ONE_DAY_OVERDUE)
        account = _make_account(customer)
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("100.00"),
            initiated_by="WRONG-CUSTOMER",
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.UNAUTHORISED


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 7 – Negative Path: Daily Limit Exceeded
# ===========================================================================

class TestDailyLimitExceeded:

    def test_outbound_declined_when_exceeds_daily_limit(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("20000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("0.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("10000.01"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.LIMIT_EXCEEDED

    def test_outbound_declined_when_partial_usage_pushes_over_limit(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("20000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("9000.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("1000.01"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.DECLINED
        assert result.decline_reason == DeclineReason.LIMIT_EXCEEDED

    def test_daily_used_not_incremented_on_limit_decline(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("20000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("9500.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,
            amount=Decimal("1000.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        service.process_transaction(req)
        assert account.daily_outbound_used == Decimal("9500.00")

    def test_limit_exceeded_message_contains_limit_info(self, service):
        customer = _make_customer(days_since_refresh=10)
        account = _make_account(
            customer,
            balance=Decimal("20000.00"),
            daily_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
            daily_used=Decimal("0.00"),
        )
        req = TransactionRequest(
            account=account,
            transaction_type=TransactionType.OUTBOUND,

        )

            amount=Decimal("50000.00"),
            initiated_by=customer.customer_id,
        )
        result = service.process_transaction(req)
        assert result.status == TransactionStatus.APPROVED

"""
Pytest test suite for KYC Refresh Requirement
Requirement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days;
             accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to
             inbound transactions only.
Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (minimal stubs – replace with real imports from your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AccountStatus(Enum):
    ACTIVE = auto()
    KYC_RESTRICTED = auto()   # beyond refresh window → inbound only
    SUSPENDED = auto()
    CLOSED = auto()


class TransactionType(Enum):
    INBOUND = auto()    # credit / deposit / incoming transfer
    OUTBOUND = auto()   # debit / withdrawal / outgoing transfer


class TransactionStatus(Enum):
    APPROVED = auto()
    DECLINED = auto()


class DeclineReason(Enum):
    KYC_REFRESH_REQUIRED = auto()
    INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS = auto()
    UNAUTHORISED = auto()
    LIMIT_EXCEEDED = auto()
    ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED = auto()
    ACCOUNT_CLOSED = auto()


@dataclass
class Customer:
    customer_id: str
    name: str
    last_kyc_refresh: datetime


@dataclass
class Account:
    account_id: str
    customer: Customer
    balance: Decimal
    status: AccountStatus = AccountStatus.ACTIVE
    daily_outbound_limit: Decimal = Decimal("10000.00")
    daily_outbound_used: Decimal = Decimal("0.00")


@dataclass
class TransactionRequest:
    account: Account
    transaction_type: TransactionType
    amount: Decimal
    initiated_by: str          # customer_id or "SYSTEM" / agent id
    reference: str = ""


@dataclass
class TransactionResult:
    status: TransactionStatus
    decline_reason: Optional[DeclineReason] = None
    new_balance: Optional[Decimal] = None
    message: str = ""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (minimal implementation – replace with real service)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

KYC_REFRESH_WINDOW_DAYS = 365


class KYCRefreshError(Exception):
    pass


class InsufficientFundsError(Exception):
    pass


class UnauthorisedError(Exception):
    pass


class LimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass


class AccountService:
    """Thin service layer that enforces KYC refresh and transaction rules."""

    def __init__(self, clock=None):
        # Allow injecting a clock for deterministic tests
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # KYC helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def is_kyc_current(self, customer: Customer) -> bool:
        age = (self._clock() - customer.last_kyc_refresh).days
        return age <= KYC_REFRESH_WINDOW_DAYS

    def days_since_kyc(self, customer: Customer) -> int:
        return (self._clock() - customer.last_kyc_refresh).days

    def apply_kyc_status(self, account: Account) -> Account:
        """Re-evaluate and update account status based on KYC freshness."""
        if account.status in (AccountStatus.SUSPENDED, AccountStatus.CLOSED):
            return account  # other statuses take precedence
        if not self.is_kyc_current(account.customer):
            account.status = AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED
        else:
            if account.status == AccountStatus.KYC_RESTRICTED:
                account.status = AccountStatus.ACTIVE
        return account

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Transaction processing
    # ---------------------------------------
